title: anodendron parviflorum
intro: Anodendron parviflorum, also known as the 'tree of death', is a type of flowering evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Borneo and the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The extracts of this plant have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Anodendron parviflorum is traditionally prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and its extracts are used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments.
